slides.title: Lights, Camera, Action…
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 5 English, Unit 9: Lights, Camera, Action…. Welcome to Unit 9! This unit is all about drama and scripts — the special kinds of writing used for plays and films. When actors perform on a stage or in front of a camera, they follow a script. A script is very different from a story in a book. Instead of long descriptions, a script uses dialogue (the words characters say), stage directions (instructions for the actors), and sometimes camera directions (instructions for how to film a scene). In this unit, you will learn how to read, understand, and write your own play scripts. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Recognise the layout and features of a play script, including character names, colons, dialogue, and stage directions
Explain the difference between a play script and a film script
Read stage directions and understand terms like upstage, downstage, centre stage, and exits/enters
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to recognise the layout and features of a play script, including character names, colons, dialogue, and stage directions. you will also explain the difference between a play script and a film script. you will also read stage directions and understand terms like upstage, downstage, centre stage, and exits/enters. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: nook, woodland, bamboo, groove, fertile, textbook. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Lights, Camera, Action…: source text 1 and Lights, Camera, Action…: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: What Are Compound Nouns?
bullets: What it means and why it matters A compound noun is a noun made from two or more words combined together to create a new meaning. Compound nouns are...
slides.narration: The first language focus is What Are Compound Nouns?. What it means and why it matters A compound noun is a noun made from two or more words combined together to create a new meaning. Compound nouns are everywhere in English. When you understand them, you can learn vocabulary much faster because you can figure out the meaning of a new word by looking at the parts inside it. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Writing 1: Write Your Own Play Script
Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Perform Your Own Script. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
